target.title: fix(webchat): broadcast agent TTS media final payloads
target.body:
## Summary
- Fixes #81468.
- Bridges WebChat-managed agent-run TTS/media supplements into the final `chat` broadcast payload, so the live WebChat path receives the audio attachment that was already appended to the transcript.
- Keeps media-bearing `block` replies from being saved as the eventual final broadcast snapshot; only actual `final` deliveries can populate that final media message.
- Preserves transcript dedupe by keeping one assistant-media transcript entry while allowing a later media final to control the live final broadcast.
- Adds running Gateway proof that sends `chat.send` over WebSocket, first delivers a media block, then delivers the final TTS media reply, verifies the assistant-media HTTP route can serve the MP3, and confirms `chat.history` still has one assistant media supplement.

## Verification
- `env COREPACK_HOME=/private/tmp/openclaw-corepack node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts -t "agent.*media|auto-TTS final media|media-bearing block" --reporter verbose`
  - `Test Files 2 passed (2)`
  - `Tests 10 passed | 166 skipped (176)`
  - `Duration 11.76s`
- `env COREPACK_HOME=/private/tmp/openclaw-corepack node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts -t "chat.send broadcasts agent-run TTS audio over the running gateway" --reporter verbose`
  - `Test Files 1 passed (1)`
  - `Tests 1 passed | 29 skipped (30)`
  - `Duration 9.50s`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Agent-run auto-TTS/audio replies could be persisted as a WebChat assistant-media transcript supplement while the live final WebChat `chat` event omitted the audio attachment. A media-bearing `block` delivery could also be captured as the final media broadcast candidate and mask a later real final media reply. This patch keeps block-media transcript compatibility but only lets actual final media deliveries populate the live final broadcast snapshot, and it keeps TTS spoken text out of the visible final/history copy.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS at head `9abfad8a60e57974ae7fe65f9d2e0f0519fd3ff9`, based on `origin/main` `23e9bc8c0b61f37851136dc5da2311935e348f5d`. I ran the repo's connected Control UI Gateway server test, which starts a real local Gateway on `127.0.0.1`, uses a real local WebSocket client to send `chat.send`, and uses real local HTTP fetches against `http://127.0.0.1:<port>/__openclaw__/assistant-media` for metadata and ticketed media download.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
- `env COREPACK_HOME=/private/tmp/openclaw-corepack node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts -t "chat.send broadcasts agent-run TTS audio over the running gateway" --reporter verbose`
- The mocked agent dispatch starts an agent run in the running Gateway, sends a media-bearing `block` reply from `block.mp3`, then sends the final TTS reply from `tts.mp3`.
- The test waits for the WebSocket final `chat` event, then calls the assistant-media metadata route and the ticketed media download route on the running Gateway.

Evidence after fix:
```txt
✓ |gateway-server| ../../src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts > gateway server chat > chat.send broadcasts agent-run TTS audio over the running gateway 1234ms

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  1 passed | 29 skipped (30)
Duration  9.50s
```

The focused handler regression also passed after the final patch:
```txt
✓ |gateway-client| ... persists agent-run audio replies emitted as media-bearing block payloads 134ms
✓ |gateway-client| ... persists auto-TTS final media as audio-only so webchat does not duplicate assistant text 31ms
✓ |gateway-client| ... broadcasts final agent media instead of an earlier media-bearing block 21ms
✓ |gateway-methods| ... persists agent-run audio replies emitted as media-bearing block payloads 81ms
✓ |gateway-methods| ... persists auto-TTS final media as audio-only so webchat does not duplicate assistant text 20ms
✓ |gateway-methods| ... broadcasts final agent media instead of an earlier media-bearing block 27ms

Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  10 passed | 166 skipped (176)
Duration  11.76s
```

Observed result after fix: The WebSocket final event arrived with `runId: idem-running-gateway-agent-tts`, `sessionKey: agent:main:main`, and `state: final`. The final assistant content included `{ type: "text", text: "Audio reply" }` plus an audio attachment for the final `tts.mp3` path with `kind: "audio"`, `mimeType: "audio/mpeg"`, and `isVoiceNote: true`. The earlier `block.mp3` payload did not replace the final `tts.mp3` broadcast. The final WebSocket message did not contain the model transcript spoken text. The assistant-media metadata request returned HTTP 200 with `available: true` and a `v1.` media ticket; the ticketed media download returned HTTP 200 and 4 bytes. `chat.history` returned exactly one assistant message for the run and did not contain the spoken TTS text. The block-only media regression asserted no final broadcast was emitted from a media block by itself.

What was not tested: No real Microsoft/Xiaomi TTS credentials were used; the test uses a local MP3 fixture while exercising the actual Gateway WebSocket final event, assistant-media HTTP route, ticketed media download, and history dedupe path. Browser autoplay behavior was not tested.

## AI Assistance Disclosure
This PR was prepared with AI assistance. I reviewed the changed code path and verified the targeted tests above.
target.author: spacegeologist
